msDesc.head: Franciscan book of hours with verses. Droitwich(?), 15th century, second quarter

msItem.title: Calendar
msItem.note: Red-letter feasts include the Translation of St Wulfstan, bishop of Worcester; the Translation of St Richard, bishop of Chichester; and the feast of St Michael in Monte Gargano.
An obit of Elinora occurs on 12 March.

msItem.title: Book of Hours
msItem.note: Leonine verses are added at each of the Hours (fols 21r–48v).
Prayers with indulgences occur at fols 65r–77v.

lg.l: Hora prima ductus est ihesus ad Pilatum.
Falsis testimoniis multum accusatum.
In collo percutiunt manibus ligatum.
Vultum eius conspuunt. lumen caeli gratum.

msItem.note: Prayers, including salutations (fol. 64v); O crux frutex saluificus (fol. 65v); Obsecro, S. Maria mater misericordie (fol. 68r).

msItem.rubric: Salutatio cum orationibus ante xv psalmos dicendos

msItem.title: Hours of the Passion
msItem.rubric: Hore de passione Domini, edite a fratre Bonauentura, apostolice sedis cardinali.
msItem.incipit: In passione domini qua salus datur lumini

msItem.title: Tractatus de ortu et uita sancte Frideswide et de miraculis eius
msItem.rubric: Incipit tractatus de ortu et uita sancte Frideswide. et de miraculis eius.
msItem.incipit: Prefuit australi plage Bretonum. sine neuo
msItem.explicit: Hunc tuearis chorum. pia mater seruula Cristi.
msItem.note: Partially printed in Benjamin Bossue, ‘De sancta Frideswida virgine, patrona Oxoniensi in Anglia’, in Joseph van Heck et al., eds, Acta sanctorum Octobris, Acta sanctorum, 55 (Brussels, 1853), 8.566–7.

msItem.title: Alliterative hexameter acrostic FRIDESVVITHA, on her virtues
msItem.rubric: Incipiunt uersus beate Frideswide insoliti coloris.

lg.l: Firma frideswida. fidei flos femina fida.
Rectos rite regens. regem reprobando rebellem.
Ignis iusticie iubar irradiauit in illa.
Dogmata digna dei dominabus dans duodenis.
Edibus ex eius enormes epulit estus.
Sacras sancta sibi. studuit sociare sodales.
Virgo uenusta uigens uirtutis uendicat usum.
Vt uiris uetitum uicium uitare uolebat.
Iudex iusticie iubar ingens intulit illi.
Turris tuta trucem tortorem tundere temptans.
Hic habitans humilis hostiles horruit hamos.
Angelicas acies. adamans ascendit ad astra.

msItem.note: Printed in Guido Maria Dreves, ed., Pia dictamina. Reimgebete und Leselieder des Mittelalters, Analecta hymnica medii aevi, 36 (Leipzig, 1905), 7.261 (no. 221). The first five lines also appear in the margin of London, British Library, Royal MS 11 D. i, fol. 72v.

msItem.title: Hexameter poem on Frideswide and Æthelthryth

lg.l: ¶ Carne frideswidam genuit rex didanus iste.
Quam tibi post genuit baptismi gratia cristi.
Clara frideswidam peperit sefrida pudicam.
Quam sibi rex regum specialem iunxit amicam.
Virgo frideswida uas morum noxiua pudoris.
Coniugium regis contempsit ipse melioris.
Spreuit etheldreda terreno nubere regi.
Diuini cultus malens se subdere legi.

msItem.author: John Pecham
msItem.title: Versus de sacramento altaris
msItem.rubric: Versus compositi a fratre Iohannem de Peccham. tocius anglie primate de sacramento altaris.

lg.l: Hostia uiua uale. fidei fons gloria matris.
Donum finale. uera figura patris.
Viuens panis aue. cum numine iuncte suaue.
Me prohibens a ue. mis memor eya faue.
Manna uiatorum. uenie uia forma sophie.
Ductor amatorum. sis michi norma uie.
Panis dulcoris. celi cibus. esca saporis.
Vas saluatoris. esto memor memoris.
Presidium domine. uirtus. plebis peregrine.
Quam deus in fine. frangere neue sine.
In te pane quidem. sunt tres. non dii. deus idem.
Sunt tres non idem. non tria. numen idem.
Duplex natura. deus es hominis caro plena.
Pane manens pura. laus tibi uerbigena.
Corpus idem numero. quondam de uirgine natum.
In cruce letatum. credo uidere mero.
Panis in altari. celo caro pura. uideris.
Visu mutari. numine non poteris.
Nil eciam pateris. indignum si comedaris.
Vt uis sic caperis. hostia sponte daris.
Tu qui dignaris. tribui nobis et in esu.
Esca salutaris. mis miserere ihesu. Amen.

msItem.note: Printed in Clemens Blume and Guido Maria Dreves, eds, Hymnographi latini: Lateinische Hymnendichter des Mittelalters, vol. 2, ed. Guido Maria Dreves, Analecta hymnica medii aevi 50 (Reisland, 1907), no. 396.

msItem.title: Prayer attributed to Augustine, to be said by the priest at Mass
msItem.rubric: Oracio Sancti Augustini dicenda a sacerdote in missa dum canitur officium ...; uel tota dicatur ante missam, quod melius est.

decoDesc.decoNote: Illuminated initials with marginal decoration at the beginning of the main parts of the book (fols 9, 29, 39, etc.).
Rubricated, with trimmed guides, such as fol. 41r (Pächt and Alexander, 3.944).

origin.origPlace: England, Droitwich Spa, Convent of Austin friars

history.provenance:
An erased inscription reads, Iste liber bige
ie. This is probably a reference to Tardebigge, 13 km from Droitwich.
James Cobbes (1602?–1685): his title page and table of contents, dated 16 January 1650 (fol. ii recto–verso).
Unidentified shelfmark, K. No. 1, 17th or 18th century (left pastedown).

recordHist.source: Description by Andrew Dunning (April 2026). Previously described in W. D. Macray, Catalogi codicum manuscriptorum Bibliothecæ Bodleianæ...viri munificentissimi Ricardi Rawlinson, J.C.D., codicum...complectens, Quarto Catalogues V, 5 fascicles, 1862–1900). Decoration, localization and date follow Pächt and Alexander (1973). Quarto Catalogue, col. 303 Summary Catalogue, vol. 3, p. 215

listBibl.head: Printed descriptions:
listBibl.bibl: S. J. P. van Dijk, Latin Liturgical Manuscripts in the Bodleian Library, Oxford, vol. 4: Books of Hours (typescript, 1957), p. 174

revisionDesc.change: Add extent, leaf dimensions and layout from van Dijk, Latin Liturgical Manuscripts in the Bodleian Library, Oxford.

Toby Burrows/Mapping Manuscript MigrationsProvenance and acquisition information added using https://github.com/mapping-manuscript-migrations/oxford-tei-updates/blob/master/update_oxford_prov.rb in collaboration with the Mapping Manuscript Migrations project.
James Cummings Up-converted the markup using https://github.com/jamescummings/Bodleian-msDesc-ODD/blob/master/convertTolkien2Bodley.xsl
